title: toona ciliata subsp. nepalensis
intro: Toona ciliata subsp. nepalensis, also known as the Himalayan cedar or Nepal cedar, is a subspecies of the Toona ciliata tree native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Toona ciliata subsp. nepalensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as an antiseptic. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to promote overall well-being and boost the immune system.
